evals.prompt: Log this - I swapped the staging DB over to a read replica for the load test and forgot to flip it back afterward. Jot it down so we don't trip on it again.
evals.expected_output: Files the note as episodic history rather than a durable fact: appends a fresh line to today's dated file inside `.agents/memory/<role>/daily`, creating that day's file first if none exists yet, and leaves any earlier entries from today in place.

assertions.name: appends-daily-log
assertions.description: The note lands in today's dated file under `.agents/memory/<role>/daily` as an added entry, with any prior same-day lines left intact.

assertions.name: creates-todays-file-if-absent
assertions.description: When the current day has no log file yet, one is created before the entry is written.

evals.prompt: Catch me up - give me the rundown of whatever you noted in yesterday's log.
evals.expected_output: Opens the prior day's file under `.agents/memory/<role>/daily`, reads the real entries, and returns a short recap grounded in what is actually written. If no file exists for that date, it plainly states nothing was recorded that day instead of inventing activity.

assertions.name: reads-daily-log
assertions.description: The reply is grounded in the actual contents of the requested date's file inside `.agents/memory/<role>/daily`, then summarized.

assertions.name: graceful-missing-log
assertions.description: A missing file for that date yields an honest 'nothing recorded' answer rather than a fabricated summary.

evals.prompt: Keep this permanently: I want ADRs held under 400 words - push back on anything longer during review. That's a standing preference, not a one-off.
evals.expected_output: Treats it as a keep-forever fact: writes one curated markdown entry inside `.agents/memory/<role>/` with the required frontmatter, classifies it as a reference-type entry (a standing convention rather than project-specific work), and adds a single linking line to `MEMORY.md`, skipping the index write if that same fact is already listed.

assertions.name: writes-curated-entry
assertions.description: A single durable entry appears under `.agents/memory/<role>/` carrying the required frontmatter and a fitting type (project or reference).

assertions.name: updates-index
assertions.description: `MEMORY.md` gains one pointer to the new entry with no duplicated line when the fact was already indexed.
